spec_match.rationale: The CDN rewrite pipeline is fully simulated using client-side JavaScript rather than executing real Service Worker rewrites and network requests.

interactive.rationale: Provides highly interactive simulated nodes and a pre/post 138 toggle showing the detailed difference in request flows.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the blurb description of showing a three-node pipeline of speculation rules and SW rewrite.

edge_cases.rationale: Successfully illustrates the comparison between pre-138 (SW bypass) and post-138 (SW handling) for multiple path types (auth, CDN edge).

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus entry and links to related concepts and the main feature page.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables and fits nicely with the design system layout.

openQuestions.title: Implement live CDN-rewrite with Service Worker
openQuestions.detail: The CDN-rewrite pipeline is currently simulated in client-side JavaScript. To make it genuinely interactive and compliant with repository rules, it should register a real local Service Worker that performs the actual header injection and URL rewrites on live speculation-rules prefetch requests, updating the UI dynamically when captured.

summary: A great simulated demonstration of SW CDN rewrites comparing pre-138 and 138 behaviors, but it could be improved by registering a real service worker and firing actual network requests.
